general_info.definition: Fluorescence resonance energy transfer system of Carbon Dots(CDs) used in a far-red fluorescence sensor for ratiometric detection of L-Cys

method.conditions: 0.05 g 4-aminobenzoic acid and 0.05 g m-phenylenediamine dissolved in 10 mL absolute ethyl alcohol then heated at 180°C for 12 h in a poly(tetrafluoroethylene)-lined autoclave

post_treatment: Dialysis (MWCO 500-1000 Da) against ultrapure water for 72 h

structural.morphology: spherical dots, well dispersed, and uniform

applications.performance: Fluorescence resonance energy transfer (FRET)-based probe for L-cysteine (L-Cys) detection with linear range of 1.0–110 μM, detection limit of 0.16 μM, and recoveries of 93–102.8% in human serum samples.
applications.comparative_advantage: The FRET fluorescent probe exhibits far-red emission (539/630 nm), reducing background and scattering interference. It shows high selectivity for L-Cys over other amino acids, with simple preparation and rapid response (3 min).
